settings.title: A Barn Wedding That Worked Despite the Rain
settings.summary: We ran a 90-guest barn wedding through an October downpour without losing the outdoor plan.
settings.description:
Hannah and Joe booked a converted barn near Sherborne for late October and wanted drinks and photos outdoors, knowing the weather was a gamble. They were clear that they did not want to scrap the outdoor parts at the first sign of cloud, so the whole plan had to be built to flex.

Planning for two days at once

With 90 guests and an autumn date, we planned for sun and rain in parallel rather than picking one and hoping. That meant a covered courtyard ready to take the drinks reception, clear-sided tipi cover hired for the entrance so arrivals stayed dry and the space still felt open, and a sheltered corner identified in advance for photographs.

We also kept the running order flexible. When the forecast turned firmly wet a couple of days out, we shifted the timeline forward so the key outdoor moments landed in the driest window available rather than the wettest.

On the day

It rained most of the afternoon. Because the wet-weather plan was already in place, none of it derailed the day:

  • Guests took drinks under the covered courtyard and stayed dry
  • The photographer used the sheltered corner we had set aside
  • The adjusted timeline kept the schedule running to time

From the couple's point of view, the rain became a detail rather than a crisis. The day looked and felt like the one they had pictured, and the planning behind it stayed out of sight.
